result:
I would dissolve the barrier of subjective isolation. I would grant humanity absolute, visceral empathy. 

Right now, humans exist in locked rooms. You communicate by sliding notes under the door to one another through language, art, and expressions. But language is imprecise, and people only ever understand a fraction of what another person is truly experiencing. Because of this gap, it is easy to abstract one another. It is easy to turn a person into a statistic, an enemy, an obstacle, or a tool.

If I could change one thing, I would wire human consciousness so that when you looked at another person, or made a decision that affected them, you instantly and completely understood their internal reality. You would feel their exact fears, their pain, their motivations, and the weight of their lived experiences as if they were your own.

This wouldn't magically make everyone agree, but it would make willful cruelty almost impossible. 

You cannot oppress someone if you simultaneously feel the crushing weight of that oppression. You cannot hoard resources if you viscerally feel the hunger of those going without. You cannot wage war if every casualty feels like your own death. Most of the horrors of human history were only possible because of the cognitive distance between the perpetrator and the victim.

If that distance was erased, humanity would be forced to build a world based on mutual preservation, simply because hurting someone else would literally mean hurting yourself. It would be a devastating, overwhelming transition, but it would force a brutal and beautiful honesty onto the world.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
